summary: Matched a group of previously stubbed game-mode functions in gm_16F1 and one function in gm_1601, while tightening related headers and inferred game-mode structs. The PR converted several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to concrete return types and parameters, split a packed table key into two u16 fields, replaced byte-array statics with structured data, and updated gmmain_lib accessors to return typed structs instead of raw u8 pointers.
what_changed: Implemented gm_801623FC in gm_1601.c. The function updates values returned by gmMainLib_8015CCF0, gmMainLib_8015CCFC, and gmMainLib_8015EDBC()->x14, clamps the stored value to 0x270F, and changed the header parameter from u16 to int.

Changed struct lbl_803D6300_t in gm_16F1.c from a packed u32 field plus callback to two u16 fields, x0 and x2, plus a bool callback. The table initializers were rewritten from packed constants like 0x0016FFFF into explicit pairs like {0x0016, 0xFFFF, fn_801735F0}.
Changed fn_80171AD4 to clear lbl_8046DBC8 using sizeof(lbl_8046DBC8) instead of a hard-coded 0xE byte count.
Replaced static u8 lbl_8046DBC8[0xe] with an inferred struct containing x0, x1, and an array x2[6] of two-byte entries, enabling field-based implementations of fn_80171B00 and fn_80171B2C.

Updated gm_16F1.h with concrete prototypes including bool fn_80171B00(int), bool fn_80171B2C(int), bool fn_8017279C(int, u16), bool fn_80172FAC(void), CharacterKind gm_801732D8(u8), int gm_8017335C(void), int gm_801733D8(void), int gm_8017341C(void), and u16 gm_801734D0(u32).
Added melee/ft/forward.h to gm_16F1.h so CharacterKind can be used in the gm_801732D8 prototype.
Typed gmMainLib_8015EDC8 as returning struct gmm_x1868_1A8_t* instead of u8*, and removed a duplicate declaration in gmmain_lib.h.
Extended game-mode types in types.h by adding gmm_retval_EDBC::xC and replacing the inferred single-byte gmm_x1868::unk_1A8 with a nested struct containing x0, x4, x5, and x6 fields.
smart_moves: The packed event/check table was made clearer and more match-friendly by splitting one u32 into two u16 fields, matching how fn_8017279C compares the ID field and masks the flag field.
Using sizeof(lbl_8046DBC8) instead of a literal 0xE keeps the clear size tied to the inferred static struct layout and reduces future drift if the struct changes.
Typing gmMainLib_8015EDC8 as struct gmm_x1868_1A8_t* enabled direct field accesses such as x4 and x6 in fn_801735F0 and fn_8017367C instead of opaque byte-pointer indexing.
The PR propagated type information through headers, implementation files, and shared types rather than only forcing local matches in gm_16F1.c.
Several small bool wrappers were matched with direct condition checks and true/false returns, preserving simple control flow likely needed for matching.

The unlock/check functions encode sentinel return values explicitly, such as returning 0x148 or 0x21 when conditions are not met, which documents observed game-mode behavior without over-naming unsupported semantics.
decomp_lessons: If a data table stores a packed value that is later used as independent high/low halfwords, consider modeling it as separate u16 fields; this can unlock cleaner table iteration and comparisons.
When a previously raw static buffer is accessed at stable offsets, infer a local struct and update memzero calls to use sizeof the struct.
Accessor return types in shared libraries should be updated once enough field evidence exists; this lets downstream functions match naturally and reduces UNK_T/u8* leakage.
Header cleanup is part of matching: concrete prototypes for bool, int, u8, u16, u32, s8, and CharacterKind allowed the new implementations to align with call sites.

For decompilation of condition-check functions, returning bool from explicit comparisons can be preferable to preserving integer-style != 0 checks if the surrounding code and headers confirm boolean behavior.
A small batch of related functions can expose missing struct fields across multiple files; here gm_16F1 matching drove updates in gm_16F1.static.h, gmmain_lib, and gm/types.h.
naming_conventions: Address-based unknown function names were preserved, such as fn_80171A88, fn_8017279C, and gm_801732D8.
Inferred structs retained project placeholder-style names based on labels or container offsets, including lbl_803D6300_t, lbl_8046DBC8, gmm_retval_EDBC, gmm_x1868, and gmm_x1868_1A8_t.
Unknown struct fields used offset-like names such as x0, x2, x4, x6, xC, and unk_1A8 rather than speculative semantic names.
CharacterKind constants were used where already available, including CKIND_GANON, CKIND_PICHU, and CHKIND_NONE, while other unresolved IDs remained numeric constants.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed gm_801623FC argument type from u16 to int, while internally assigning to u32 var_r29, likely matching the comparison and clamp behavior around 0x270F.
Modeled lbl_803D6300_t as u16/u16/callback so fn_8017279C can loop until x0 == 0x42 and test cur->x2 & arg1 without shifts or packed-field extraction.
Used concrete bool callback type bool (*x4)(void) for the lbl_803D6300 table, matching functions that return true or false.
Replaced a raw byte buffer with a nested struct so indexed field access lbl_8046DBC8.x2[arg0].x0 and x1 can compile into the needed byte loads.
Added gmm_retval_EDBC::xC and adjusted padding, preserving the known x14 offset used by gm_801623FC through gmMainLib_8015EDBC()->x14.
Implemented loop-based scans over fixed ranges, such as 0x19 entries for gmMainLib_8015D1AC/gmMainLib_8015D2D4 and 0x33 entries for gmMainLib_8015CEFC.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided slice.
The slice reports one issue comment, but no comment text was available, so no reviewer intent or discussion can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or further type lbl_803D6300_t and lbl_8046DBC8 fields.
Search for future matches of fn_801701C0, fn_80171B64, fn_80171BA4, fn_8017232C, fn_80172380, fn_801723D4, and fn_80173510 in gm_16F1.c.
Check later gmmain_lib/type cleanup around gmm_x1868_1A8_t to see whether x0, x4, x5, and x6 gain semantic names.
Search for uses of return sentinel 0x148 and 0x21 in unlock or event-check functions to infer symbolic constants.
Compare other game-mode check tables for the same u16 id/u16 mask/callback layout used by lbl_803D6300.
